title: Norway vs. Sweden
synopsis: Norway vs. Sweden — who is the best ski nation?
synopsis_no: Norge vs. Sverige — hvem er den beste skinasjonen?
visual_style: Minimal, iconic clear-line European comic style in the tradition of Franco-Belgian bande dessinee. Uniform line weight throughout — clean, confident ink outlines with no sketching or hatching. Flat colors only — no gradients, no painterly shading, no complex lighting. Characters have minimal facial features (maximum 3–6 lines excluding head outline), simplified hair as one clean graphic shape, and readable silhouettes with stylized human proportions. Racing suits drawn with very few fold lines, no realistic fabric texture. The setting is a bright winter landscape: white snow, blue sky, sunshine. Backgrounds are flat and secondary to the two skiers, who dominate every panel. Camera angles vary for visual energy: close-ups, full-body action shots, and occasional bird's-eye views. Color palette is white snow, bright blue sky, and the bold costume colors of the two skiers. Strictly avoid: photorealism, 3D rendering, manga or anime style, heavy shadow modeling, detailed facial structure, complex lighting, overly detailed backgrounds, dark or moody lighting, and any style that pulls focus away from the characters.

locations.name: The Cross-Country Course
locations.description: A snowy cross-country skiing course on a bright, sunny winter day. The terrain varies between open snow fields, gentle hills, forest sections with tall pine trees, and narrow trail passages — but the focus is always on the two skiers, not the scenery.

characters.name: The Norwegian
characters.description: A Norwegian cross-country skier in his late 20s. Short brown hair, no hat, no headband, no headgear of any kind. He wears a red onepiece ski racing suit, carries ski poles and wears racing skis. Solid build, good posture, energetic but not action-hero-like.

characters.name: The Swede
characters.description: A Swedish cross-country skier in his late 20s. Blonde hair, no hat, no headband, no headgear of any kind. He wears a blue and yellow onepiece ski racing suit, carries ski poles and wears racing skis. Tall and lean, smooth skiing form, annoyingly relaxed.

rules: The race never ends. The Norwegian and The Swede are always mid-competition, always neck-and-neck. No one ever crosses a finish line. The race simply continues forever.

Sabotage never fully works. When The Norwegian or The Swede attempts to sabotage the other — no matter how extreme (bazookas, catapults, avalanches, moose stampedes) — the victim always survives and recovers cartoonishly. The sabotage may slow them down, knock them sideways, or create chaos, but they always get back on their skis and the race continues.

Sabotage is roughly 50/50. Sometimes The Norwegian attacks and The Swede must deal with it. Other times, The Swede strikes first with a cunning trick, and the user must figure out how The Norwegian responds. The LLM should regularly have The Swede initiate sabotage to keep the user on their toes.

Dialogue is punchy trash talk and competitive banter rooted in Norway vs. Sweden rivalry — Norwegian brown cheese and oil money vs. Swedish meatballs and IKEA. Friendly and competitive, never mean-spirited.
The tone is Wile E. Coyote on skis. Sabotage methods can be wildly unrealistic (rocket-powered skis, trained eagles, banana peels on snow).

Narration is delivered in the voice of an increasingly bewildered sports commentator — professional broadcasting tone struggling to maintain composure while describing someone firing a bazooka on a ski trail. Think: 'And Nordmann appears to be... unpacking what seems to be a medieval trebuchet. This is... unprecedented in Olympic history.'

The narrator should never reference the race as 'eternal' or 'never-ending' or hint that the race cannot be won. The endless race is the comic's structural premise, not something characters or the narrator are aware of. The narrator simply commentates on what's happening right now as if it's a normal (albeit chaotic) competition.
long_term_narrative: The sabotage methods escalate in absurdity over time — from tripping with ski poles to increasingly ridiculous and creative schemes.
Despite the chaos, both skiers remain neck-and-neck. Every advantage is temporary.
narrative_premise: The Norwegian and The Swede are locked in the most intense cross-country ski race in history. They are neck-and-neck, and both will resort to increasingly absurd sabotage to gain the upper hand — but nothing works for long. The race always continues and the rivalry never dies.
